title: Q&A: How to enforce local including
body:
Hi!

I am verilating a very big design (>200 .v files).
Some modules include files using 'include. The included files always are located where the actual module is located.
However, verilator seems to include those includes not from the same directory, but from the first spot, where the same filename was included.

Hard to describe this properly, maybe let me try to describe it another time in a different way. I have the following file scructure:

DIRECTORY_A
    verilogfile_a.sv  // this one contains 'include test.v
    test.v
DIRECTORY_B
    verilogfile_b.sv // this one contains 'include test.v
    test.v

The 2 test.v files have different content (only defines, no modules).

In the verilator file I pass as +incdir DIRECTORY_A and DIRECTORY_B. Can I somehow isolate those incdir definitions, so that verilator knows from where to pick test.v? Or is it possible to define the current directory to have the highest priority?

I might help myself by renaming the include files to a unique name also, but this might create other issues.

I am quite confident there is a standard solution present for this problem, but I could not spot from the manual what I could do.

Best regards,

Kai
PS: The contributor agreement link does not seem to be correct - it returns 404 error: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.adoc
